role: This file is the operator-facing React surface and local controller for reviewing and batch-approving registry-generation proposals before they are written back to the registry.
capabilities: Fetch a proposed registration batch via GET /api/registry/proposals
Render each proposal as a plain-language dossier with howto trio, capabilities, and no-fiction grounding tags
Group proposals by parent address or mockup file for screen-level review
Allow per-entry skip/keep toggle with transient local state
Submit one batch approve via POST /api/registry/approve with the skipped set excluded
Surface backend errors and approve notices fail-loud
Inject or self-host the useRegistryProposals controller hook
substance_why: The file contains complete TypeScript domain types, a self-contained data hook with fetch/approve handlers, grouped rendering logic, and a full JSX review surface wired to real interactions.
interactions_present: Refresh button re-fetches the proposal batch
Per-dossier skip/keep toggle updates transient local skip set
Batch approve button commits the kept proposals
Visual tags for feature mapping and grounding state
interactions_missing: No explicit reject/close action in the UI despite the consent model mentioning it
No search, filter, or sort for large batches
No pagination or virtualization for long proposal lists
No retry or dismiss for error/notice banners
No per-entry expand/detail or edit capability
No confirmation before the destructive approve write-back
performance_risks: Groups and item counts are recomputed on every render with no memoization
No virtualization; large proposal arrays will render many DOM nodes and cards
Inline style objects and callbacks are recreated each render, increasing re-render churn
approve() calls refresh() sequentially, causing an extra fetch+render cycle after commit
No request abort/timeout on the initial fetch or refresh
reuse_why: The RG8 review-and-approve concept, no-fiction tags, and simple-consent batch gate are well-defined, but the hook, grouping, and card rendering should be decoupled and hardened during redesign.
decomposition_hints: Move useRegistryProposals into a dedicated hooks file and separate it from the presentational component
Split DossierCard, FeatureTag, and GroundingTag into their own component files
Extract grouping logic into a memoized utility or selector
Extract the approve-bar footer into a standalone BatchActions component
Move domain types (RegistryDossier, ProposalBatch) to a shared types module
